description: Odd-but-legal exports: nested subsheets (Main → Level One → Level Two), choice routing, unknown stage types (ProcessInfo/SubSheetInfo), a stage whose page was deleted, a page reference to a nonexistent page, an unknown data type, a variable queue name, an empty collection definition, a plain-text code body, and all six alternative App Modeller modes plus one unknown mode. The parser must tolerate all of it: 7 warnings, 0 errors, nothing dropped silently.
notes: Expected warnings (7): unknown stage type ProcessInfo; stray subsheetid on data item 'Drifted Item' (attached to Main Page); non-literal Queue Name on 'Queue Audit Row'; unknown stage type SubSheetInfo; 'Ghost Page' reference to nonexistent page (left unresolved); unknown data type 'fancytype' (treated as text); unknown app element mode 'Mainframe' (treated as Win32). Zero findings expected: the process has Recover/Resume coverage, every data item is referenced, the queued collection has no PII fields, and warnings must never be double-reported as findings.
